completion_criteria_checked: The 'no new behavior invented' claim was independently refined and confirmed accurate: process-rollover-requests.ps1 adds new request-file workflow behavior (scan, validate, skip malformed, capture output, mark processed, move) around exactly one existing script (safe-stop.ps1), but invents no new rollover/reset logic itself -- matching the task's actual requirement (no new rollover/reset behavior beyond composing the existing check), not the looser 'no new behavior at all' phrasing.

scripts/safe-stop.ps1 confirmed genuinely read-only and consistent with its own stated header: composes validate-cockpit-state.ps1 and verify-dual-restart-safety.ps1, reads state, prints a summary, always exits 0, no write path found in the file itself. Reviewer notes a single file read cannot prove it is unmodified across all history, but this cycle's repo truth (invoked, not edited; boundaries forbade editing it) is consistent with that claim.

All three claimed test scenarios (successful end-to-end process, empty-inbox no-op, malformed-file tolerance) independently confirmed genuinely backed by the code, matching worker-result.md's account exactly.
evidence_reviewed: scripts/request-rollover.ps1 (full file, read via remote repo access)
risks: Disclosed, non-blocking: the consumer performs lightweight structural validation (required fields present) rather than full JSON Schema validation against schemas/request.schema.json. Reviewer judged this consistent with the task's own completion criteria, though lighter than full schema validation -- worth tightening in a future task if request-file volume or diversity grows.

This verification was performed via the ChatGPT manual bridge (DECISION-086) with remote, read-only repo access only -- no local execution, no independent re-run of scripts/verify-handback-guardrails.ps1 or other local guardrails. Per docs/VERIFICATION_RESULT_SPEC.md, this is additive review, not a substitute for local-guardrail-based independent verification.
summary: The rollover producer/consumer pair does exactly what it claims: no new rollover/reset logic was invented, only new request-file workflow behavior wrapped around the existing, confirmed-unmodified, confirmed-read-only scripts/safe-stop.ps1. All three claimed test scenarios are genuinely backed by the code. No blocker found; one minor, disclosed, non-blocking note about lightweight (not full-schema) request validation.
next_action: Mark task complete. Next Phase D3 task: pcc-pathD-009 (tone/behavior controls), the last task named in docs/PATH_A_PLAN.md section 6's current Phase D3 scope.
